title: Alias field-store source propagation
tests_pre_existing_note: Issue #13 named these two test functions; grep -rn across crates/tldr-core/ on HEAD 88ddac6 returned zero matches. Tests written from scratch per issue body's bug description and reproduction requirements.

red_evidence.test_field_store_simple_no_phi: expected points-to set for param_obj.field to contain {param_a}; got: {} (alias-set mismatch — VAL-005/issue #13: source-propagation through field store missing)
red_evidence.test_field_store_source_propagation_thro: expected points-to set for param_obj.field to contain alloc_3; got: {} (alias-set mismatch — VAL-005/issue #13: source-propagation through phi missing)

green_evidence.field_store_tests: 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 5059 filtered out
green_evidence.full_alias_suite: 127 passed; 0 failed; 34 ignored; 0 measured; 4900 filtered out

matrix.note: exhaustive_matrix run with --test-threads=1 due to transient embedding-mutex contention documented in M4 prompt (multi-threaded shows 676/730 with 54 transients that pass on retry or in isolation)

fix_summary.index_changed: Constraint::FieldStore branch in index_constraints now populates reverse_field_stores[source] = [(base, field)] (replacing spurious reverse_copy entry that was never acted upon)
fix_summary.branch_added: propagate_variable now re-runs propagate_field_store for every (base, field) in reverse_field_stores[var] when var is propagated, restoring Andersen's pts(loc.field) ⊇ pts(source) inclusion soundness
